genetic_lineage.overview: Falanghina Beneventana is indigenous to Campania. DNA analysis confirms it is distinct from Falanghina Flegrea, which was previously considered the same variety.

parents.name: Unknown
parents.notes: No parentage established. Indigenous to Campania, possibly dating to Roman era.

siblings.name: Falanghina Flegrea
siblings.notes: DNA confirms these are separate varieties despite historical treatment as one. Falanghina Flegrea is found in Campi Flegrei.

genetic_lineage.genetic_significance: The distinction between Falanghina Beneventana and Falanghina Flegrea demonstrates how DNA analysis continues to clarify Italian ampelography. Both varieties are now recognized as distinct genetic entities.

clones.name: Clone ISV-F1
clones.characteristics: Standard certified clone with good aromatic intensity and bitter almond finish.

clones.name: Clone ISV-F2
clones.characteristics: Selected for enhanced floral aromatics and freshness.

clones.name: Taburno Selection
clones.characteristics: Massal selections from the Taburno hills. More structured wines with aging potential.

clones.name: Benevento Heritage Selection
clones.characteristics: Old vine selections preserving traditional character. Intense aromatics with pronounced bitter almond.

terroir.effect: Common throughout Campania. Produces wines with mineral intensity and aromatic complexity. The volcanic origin contributes to the variety's distinctive character.

wine_character.aromatics: Citrus, white flowers, herbs, bitter almond

terroir.effect: Found in parts of Sannio. Produces rounder, more textured wines with softer acidity.
Similar to Greco di Tufo soils. Produces more mineral, age-worthy wines.

susceptibilities.notes: Can be affected in humid conditions.

disease_profile.general_notes: Falanghina is a mid-ripening variety that maintains good acidity in warm climates. The variety's aromatic profile develops best with careful temperature-controlled fermentation. The characteristic bitter almond finish distinguishes it from other Italian whites and is most pronounced in wines from volcanic soils.

regional_expressions.style_summary: The heartland of Falanghina production around Benevento. Produces fresh, aromatic wines with characteristic floral notes and bitter almond finish. Quality has improved dramatically with modern winemaking techniques.

typical_character.aromatics: Citrus, white flowers, peach, bitter almond, herbs

regional_expressions.style_summary: Hillside appellation producing more structured Falanghina with greater aging potential. The elevation and soil diversity create wines of more complexity than typical Sannio examples.

typical_character.aromatics: Citrus, white flowers, stone fruit, mineral, bitter almond

regional_expressions.style_summary: Regional designation used by producers seeking flexibility. Some innovative winemakers use it for skin-contact or barrel-fermented versions. Quality varies but can offer good value.
